title: Pelle Calls, Larsson Cuts
objective: Survive a 2-4 minute play-call sprint by memorizing and replaying pulse sequences, adapting whenever the call flips from normal to mirrored or reversed.

controls.input: Hold the phone vertically and watch the four floor panels light with matching ch
controls.outcome: You learn the current Pelle sequence and its timing.

controls.input: Tap the lit floor panels in sequence with one or two thumbs
controls.outcome: Larsson completes the play, scores points, and builds combo.

controls.input: Watch the center badge flash FORWARD, MIRROR, or REVERSE every few rounds
controls.outcome: You know whether to repeat the pattern as heard, swap left/right lanes, or enter it backward.

controls.input: Hit the final glowing panel on the beat
controls.outcome: You bank the round multiplier instead of dropping part of the combo.

steps: A short four-panel court appears, labeled with Pelle on the call side and Larsson on the cut side.
Pelle broadcasts a pulse pattern with sharp lights, sneaker squeaks, claps, and bass hits.
The player re-enters the pattern on touch panels before the shot clock ring empties.
Every 2-3 rounds, a whistle twist changes the rule: play it forward, mirrored left-to-right, or in reverse.
Successful replays add one beat, tighten the timer, and speed up the audio.
One mistake, a timeout on the clock, or an off-beat final hit costs the run.
scoring: Each correct panel is worth points, perfect-on-beat taps raise a combo multiplier, and finishing a twisted round without errors banks a bonus. Leaderboards rank highest total score from a single run.
tips: Learn the sound first; each panel has its own instrument so you can memorize without staring.
Mirror rounds only swap the lane positions, not the rhythm spacing.
Reverse rounds are safer if you mentally lock the last two beats first.
Banking a twisted round is worth more than rushing a sloppy long pattern.
